A.missing: no second thing; only one thing touching something, no join of two; A-LONG-TIME is idle
A.reason: One thing touches something for a long time; misses joining two things together and the resulting fixed state.

B.reason: Agent acts on two things so they touch and cannot move — renders act of fastening two things together, minor genericity.

C.reason: Agent acts on two things and afterward they touch and cannot not-touch (stay joined); captures the fastening act, double-negation clumsy but sound.

D.missing: resulting fixity given via cannot-move-far rather than cannot-separate
D.reason: Because agent does something the thing touches another and afterward cannot move far — renders the act and its fixed result.

E.missing: no agent doing; things just HAPPEN to touch, not an act of fastening
E.reason: Agentless: two things exist and something happens so they touch — misses the act of someone fastening them.

F.missing: no agent/DO; state-change described without anyone fastening
F.reason: Describes before-not-touching then touching and fixed, but agentless — the fastening as an act performed is absent.

G.missing: resulting fixity (cannot move apart) not asserted, only that they touch
G.reason: Someone does something with two things so they touch — renders the joining act, though permanence of hold is only implicit.
